steps.title: Set up the equation using exponent rules
steps.content:
Write the function values: $$f(7)=c^7$$ and $$f(5)=c^5$$.
Substitute into the given condition $$f(7)=9\cdot f(5)$$: $$c^7 = 9 \cdot c^5$$.
Since $$c>0$$, divide both sides by $$c^5$$: $$c^2 = 9$$.

steps.title: Solve for c and select the answer
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides: $$c = \sqrt{9} = 3$$ (the negative root is excluded because $$c>1$$).
The answer is 3.
